video_title: The Future of Finance Starts Onchain | Armani Ferrante
gist: Armani Ferrante (Backpack, creator of Anchor/Serum-era Solana tooling) argues the future of finance is a fully-regulated, crypto-native institution rather than the regulatory-arbitrage middle ground, and that Backpack's edge is being the first to issue 1:1 redeemable tokenized equities (SpaceX, Micron) with weekend/24-7 price discovery on Solana, a unified portfolio-margin exchange built like a blockchain, and 'strictly superior' tokenomics with no team allocation locked until a year post-IPO.

themes.theme: Regulated vs. decentralized: the collapsing middle ground

insights.claim: Crypto financial businesses will bifurcate into two viable end-states, fully regulated compliant institutions or fully decentralized censorship-resistant protocols, and the profitable regulatory-arbitrage middle ground will not exist in 10 years.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Signals a strategic bet that hybrid/arbitrage exchanges are structurally doomed; positions Backpack on the regulated extreme.
insights.quote: you're either going to be kind of uh uh one of two things ... a fully regulated compliant financial institution ... or you're going to be a fully decentralized censorship resistant ... protocol

insights.claim: Binance's June 2026 exit from Europe (failing to get MiCA across the finish line) is a proof point that even a firm with 10x-100x the resources of the next competitor cannot survive in the middle ground, implying smaller arbitrage players have no chance.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Uses Binance-Europe as a template for how mid-ground exchanges get forced to pick a path or lose markets.
insights.quote: this week Binance announced that it's pulling out of Europe ... with infinite amount of resources you can't get live in in Europe

insights.claim: Backpack is the only new post-FTX exchange that put regulation front-and-center from the first trade, with Coinbase and Kraken cited as the only comparable regulated peers.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Frames Backpack's moat as regulatory infrastructure that rivals skipped for speed.
insights.quote: we're really the only new exchange in a post FDX era that took this path. There's there's not a single other one that you can uh uh that you can name

insights.claim: Kalshi taking prediction-market category leadership over Polymarket despite a slower start illustrates that the fully-regulated route can win long-term even at the cost of near-term growth.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Supports the thesis that early regulatory discipline compounds into a category lead.
insights.quote: you can see Kalshi just becoming the market category leader right now ... they started much more slowly

themes.theme: Tokenized equities and 24/7 onchain price discovery

insights.claim: Backpack Securities was the first to ever issue a tokenized stock that is not cash-settled but gives a 1:1 redeemable right to the underlying security entitlement, requiring in-house brokerage, KYC, custodian, exchange, and issuing entity.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Differentiates Backpack's product from cash-settled synthetic tokenized-stock competitors.
insights.quote: the very first to you know ever issue a tokenized stock that was not cash settled but actually gave you a onetoone redeemable right for the underlying security entitlement

insights.claim: Backpack's north star is 24/7 spot-stock trading, concentrating all liquidity and market-maker attention on a small set of high-attention assets (e.g. SpaceX) to build the deepest weekend price-discovery markets rather than listing thousands of symbols.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Explains the deliberate 'few deep symbols' listing cadence versus a broad-catalog Robin Hood-style approach.
insights.quote: the northstar is 247 trading ... You focus all of your energy on the assets that have the attention and the assets that matter

insights.claim: Backpack demonstrated buying Micron Technology on NASDAQ and withdrawing it to Solana in roughly 20 seconds, showing native crypto-speed settlement bridging TradFi and onchain.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Concrete latency proof point that CEX-to-chain equity withdrawal works at token speed.
insights.quote: literally see you buying Micron Technology on NASDAQ and then withdrawing it to Salana in I think it was like 20 seconds just at at the speed of a token

insights.claim: Backpack can already offer thousands of stocks via its brokerage and could open onchain withdrawals for all of them tomorrow via an intent-solver system bridging a native brokerage, but is deliberately choosing depth over breadth for now.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Reveals a latent 'whole US stock market onchain' capability gated by go-to-market choice, not technology.
insights.quote: Go on a backpack right now and trade thousands of stocks through a brokerage. If you really want to have your portfolio on chain ... you need to open up all all these symbols ... Not hundreds, but thousands

insights.claim: If a tokenized asset isn't achieving 24/7 native decentralized crypto execution, there's no point doing RWAs at all, since regulated brokerages already exist for normal-hours trading.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: A sharp bar for RWA products: onchain-native execution is the only defensible reason to tokenize.
insights.quote: if you're not doing that like why are you doing RWAS in the first place is kind of my position

insights.claim: Tokenized spot equities export local equities globally the way stablecoins export the US dollar, giving worldwide investors access to US and Asian (Korea, Japan, Hong Kong) AI-infrastructure names.
insights.speaker: Danny / Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Frames tokenized equities as a global demand-capture play mirroring stablecoin dollar dominance.
insights.quote: what a stable coin really is ... it's really like you know exporting the US dollar to the entire world

themes.theme: Exchange built like a blockchain: trust-minimized CeFi

insights.claim: Backpack's exchange is architected as a multi-node operator network with a deterministic state machine over a global linear log of ED25519-signed transactions, allowing anyone to replay and verify every order, fill, cancel and liquidation from genesis.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Verifiable state machine is the technical basis for daily proof-of-reserves and trust minimization.
insights.quote: the whole exchange is built like a blockchain ... a multi-node operator network with a deterministic state machine built off of glo off a global linear log of authenticated transactions

insights.claim: Backpack claims to be the only exchange doing daily proof-of-reserves (verifiable via open-source code and a zero-knowledge proof), versus competitors doing it monthly or quarterly.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Positions Backpack as 'most trust-minimized regulated financial institution' as a marketing and safety differentiator.
insights.quote: we can build not only a highly secure system, but also do stuff like daily proof reserves. We're the only exchange on the planet that does this

insights.claim: AI-driven risk (frontier models potentially breaking open smart contracts with no recourse) will place a growing premium on formal verification, immutability, and provable safety of user funds.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Suggests DeFi security scrutiny intensifies and favors verifiably safe custody models.
insights.quote: there's just going to be a huge premium on like things like formal verification and immutability and uh uh the safety of users funds

themes.theme: Unified portfolio margin and global fiat rails

insights.claim: Backpack unifies spot, spot margin, borrow-lending, perpetual futures, crypto, fiat on/off ramp, and now stocks into a single portfolio-margin account with instant settlement on crypto rails.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Cross-asset unified margin (including tokenized stocks as collateral) is the core trading differentiator.
insights.quote: spot, spot margin, borrow lending, perpetual futures, crypto, fiat on and off ramping, now stocks, all unified in a single portfolio

insights.claim: Backpack offers organic borrow-and-stablecoin-yield of up to 6% APY (VIP6) or 7% APY (VIP5) usable as trading collateral, with no incentivized emissions or gimmicks.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Concrete yield figures signal a native, non-subsidized lending pool baked into the exchange.
insights.quote: you can get 6% APY if you're VIP six or 7% APY on VIP5 and that's purely from borrow interest and purely from stable coin yield

insights.claim: Users will soon be able to borrow dollars against tokenized SpaceX stock and spend instantly, a use case only possible with crypto rails, instant settlement, and solved regulatory challenges.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Points to consumer credit/spend products collateralized by tokenized equities as a near-term frontier.
insights.quote: be able to borrow against your SpaceX stock and get dollars and like withdraw it to like ... tap your phone and pay for dinner

insights.claim: Zero-fee international USD wires and true FX spot markets (e.g. a native JPY/USD pair for remittances) are rare among international crypto exchanges and are a key differentiator Backpack built by working with local regulators.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Multi-jurisdiction fiat rails (yen, euro, AED, USD) are a hard-to-replicate on/off-ramp moat.
insights.quote: being able to wire from a USD bank account anywhere around the world, being able to do that for zero fees

insights.claim: Backpack has spent 3.5-4 years working with the JFSA in Japan and established local teams/offices in Japan, Europe, UAE, US, and Australia, a regulatory footprint few competitors will replicate.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Quantifies the multi-year, multi-country licensing effort underpinning the moat.
insights.quote: I'm calling you from Tokyo right now. We've been working, you know, with the GFSA here in Japan for, you know, three and a half, four years

themes.theme: Tokenomics: no team allocation, IPO-gated unlock

insights.claim: The BP token has no team, founder, executive, or investor allocation; a frozen portion sits publicly on Backpack's balance sheet and only unlocks one year after an IPO, which Ferrante calls the most extreme tokenomics ever created.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Aligns insiders to a decade-scale build and removes the dump-on-retail failure mode as a marketing/trust edge.
insights.quote: it's not even at the IPO it's a year after IPO do people get access uh uh to those tokens

insights.claim: Every token could be made 'strictly superior' by attaching even 1 basis point of equity in the labs entity, eliminating the failure mode where token holders get nothing if the company is acquired.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Proposes a token-plus-equity design pattern that de-risks acquisition scenarios versus naked tokens.
insights.quote: it could literally be one BIP uh uh and it would be strictly better than every token that exists uh uh on planet Earth

insights.claim: Tokens are the best-ever tool to bootstrap network effects but the worst incentive mechanism for building enduring companies, evidenced by nearly all world-changing crypto firms (Coinbase, Tether, Circle) being equity projects, not token projects.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: A skeptical framing of token-first companies that informs Backpack's equity-plus-token structure.
insights.quote: tokens are just the worst possible incentive mechanism for anybody that's trying to build something real

insights.claim: The dominant low-float / high-FDV / skewed 'fugazi' tokenomics that trend to zero must end; Ferrante hopes Backpack's model raises the bar for higher-quality assets entering crypto.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Signals a coming market repricing away from extractive launch structures.
insights.quote: Lowflow, high FTV, skewed tokconomics, bunch of fugazi sprinkled in the middle, and you see a down only chart

insights.claim: Post-IPO, Ferrante expects a Binance/Coinbase-style dual structure where a public equity business coexists with a token/decentralized network (e.g. Coinbase almost certainly launching a Base token).
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Predicts equity-plus-network as the standard model and flags a Base token as a likely near-term event.
insights.quote: You have Coinbase ... They have Bass. They're certainly going to do a token with Bass

themes.theme: Market structure: why RWAs migrate onchain

insights.claim: Traditional securities settlement is broken because each intermediary (introducing/executing/clearing broker, DTCC, transfer agent, NASDAQ) keeps a different ledger, forcing constant reconciliation and T+1/T+2 settlement versus 200-400ms on Solana.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: A blockchain as single canonical ledger removes reconciliation and settlement lag, enabling capital-efficient margin/risk systems.
insights.quote: each one of these parties have different books ... this is what creates issues ... something as simple as settlement ... money is moving on the order of days

insights.claim: Brokers are legally authorized to sell shares they don't actually have; the Dole Food case, where more holders claimed shares than existed on the books after a shareholder lawsuit win, exposes the fragility a canonical blockchain ledger would eliminate.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Vivid evidence that intermediated ledgers over-issue ownership, a systemic risk blockchains resolve.
insights.quote: more people showed up and claimed that they had the stock than was actually recorded on the books of the company ... brokers are authorized to sell shares of stocks that they don't actually have

insights.claim: DTCC's ongoing work will fundamentally change US market structure, and building durable tokenized products requires understanding that evolution rather than copying FTX-era Liechtenstein prospectus templates.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Warns that copycat tokenized-stock products lack durable first-principles design.
insights.quote: you saw a a lot of these products literally just copy and paste the exact same Likenstein perspectus white paper um that FTX used to build their product

insights.claim: RWAs and crypto-native tokens have been in extreme disequilibrium because the RWA barrier was historically high; as regulation lands, assets rebalance and the world returns to a rational state with far fewer memecoins dominating.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Predicts a structural shift in onchain asset mix toward real assets and higher-bar crypto-native tokens.
insights.quote: there's been this huge unnatural disproportionate like you know uh uh quantity of of assets in one category relative to the other. And now what we're seeing is those things come into equilibrium

insights.claim: Once the entire economy runs on decentralized blockchains via finance and RWAs, the prerequisite is unlocked to revisit earlier 'too early' decentralization experiments (decentralized Uber, inference, DePIN) that failed like MySpace before Facebook.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Frames finance as the foundational layer that later enables a second wave of non-financial decentralized apps.
insights.quote: the early experiments, they were just early ... It's like you're building Fster ... or MySpace before Facebook came out

insights.claim: Backpack originally left the US (from Chicago) because bootstrapping a new regulated crypto exchange domestically with limited resources was infeasible, and has spent nearly four years trying to go live in the US.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Explains the offshore-first strategy and the pending US market entry as a key catalyst.
insights.quote: we moved outside of the us because we just didn't see a way that you could actually bootstrap a uh you know a new regulated crypto exchange from scratch

insights.claim: CFTC and SEC actions under the current US administration are treated as a genuine commitment to onshore crypto and preserve US capital-market dominance, a primary input enabling the tokenized-equities wave.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Regulatory clarity is framed as the unlock that makes previously-impossible products viable for a new founder generation.
insights.quote: everything that the CFTC and the SEC is doing in Washington ... it's ... a testament to ... the genuine commitment to bring crypto on shore

insights.claim: Just as stablecoins exported the US dollar globally, US company securities will see the same global-demand dynamic as everyone seeks access to high-quality US businesses onchain.
insights.speaker: Armani Ferrante
insights.so_what: Bullish structural demand thesis for tokenized US equities as a dollar-dominance analog.
insights.quote: everybody wants access to the US, everybody wants access to kind of these companies ... to invest uh uh into highquality businesses that really are the envy of the world
